data.title: Why Is Psyence Biomedical Stock (PBM) Up Today?
data.description:
Psyence Biomedical (PBM) stock continued to rally on Friday following the release of a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This SEC filing was focused on an expansion in Australia for its clinical site network. The company has expanded the number of these locations to five from three, allowing it to better conduct clinical trials.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Psyence Biomedical noted that its ongoing Phase IIb clinical trial of natural psilocybin for the treatment of Adjustment Disorder in patients with cancer in a palliative care setting will directly benefit from this expansion. The company expects it to strengthen recruitment and accelerate enrollment. Psyence Biomedical has provided its pharmaceutical-grade psilocybin product, NPX-5, for this trial.

Jody Aufrichtig, CEO of Psyence BioMed, said, "This expansion marks an important step forward in scaling the clinical infrastructure required to advance NPX-5 and support ongoing patient dosing. By adding high-quality clinical partners such as Ramsay Health Care and NeuroCentrix, we are strengthening our ability to execute efficiently, accelerate enrollment, and generate meaningful clinical data. As patients continue to be dosed with NPX-5, our focus remains on advancing the program through a disciplined, regulatory-aligned pathway."

Psyence Biomedical Stock Movement Today

Psyence Biomedical stock was up 69.85% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 103.82% rally yesterday. The stock has also increased 24.68% year-to-date but was still down 73.1% over the past 12 months.

PBM stock experienced heavy trading activity alongside today's rally. This saw some 23.9 million shares change hands this morning, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 1.12 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: AMD Stock Torpedoes Resistance with All-Time High after Inking Historic France Government AI Deal
data.description:
The global race for artificial intelligence has a new frontrunner as a massive partnership in Europe rattles the financial world. Yesterday, AMD (AMD) and the French government announced a historic deal to transform France into a global AI leader. The news acted as fuel for the market, sending AMD stock surging 7.8% to a record-shattering close of $278.26 and marking its 12th consecutive day of gains.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

AMD and France Create a Sovereign AI Giant

The new partnership is designed to give French researchers and startups the tools they need to build the next generation of digital intelligence.

At the heart of the deal is a plan to give local developers and students massive access to AMD's most advanced computing power. The two sides signed a formal agreement at the Ministry of Economy in Paris to support France's national goal of becoming an AI superpower.

Sébastien Martin, the Minister Delegate for Industry, noted that this collaboration is a major step toward "technological sovereignty." This means France wants to control its own digital future rather than relying on outside tech giants. AMD is effectively building the foundation for a new European AI ecosystem through the provision of hardware, software, and deep technical training.

Supercomputer "Alice Recoque" Powers Future Research

The most impactful part of yesterday's announcement is the plan to unlock the full power of France's first-ever "Exascale" supercomputer.

Named Alice Recoque, this massive machine will be powered by AMD technology and will act as the brain for the country's most complex research projects. To make sure the country gets the most out of this hardware, AMD is helping to create a "Center of Excellence." This hub will provide expert training to help scientists and engineers use the supercomputer to solve the world's biggest challenges.

Philippe Baptiste, the Minister of Higher Education, stated that this gives French researchers "unprecedented access to cutting-edge infrastructures," which will help them compete on the world stage.

Global AI Markets Expand through Open Ecosystems

While the deal is focused on France, it sends a clear signal to the rest of the world that the demand for AI chips is only getting stronger.

Adding to this, AMD executive Keith Strier praised France for having one of the most ambitious AI programs in Europe. He explained that AMD is providing the workbench that will allow French innovators to push the limits of what AI can do. This partnership is about creating an open system where anyone can build and share new software.

Furthermore, Anne Le Hénanff, the Minister for AI and Digital Affairs, reminded the public that "there is no AI without infrastructure." Because AMD is now providing that backbone for a major world power, investors are betting that the company will continue to see massive growth throughout 2026.

Is AMD Stock a Buy or Sell?

Turning to TipRanks, AMD has a Moderate Buy consensus rating among Wall Street analysts as of April 2026. This rating is based on 20 Buy and eight Hold ratings issued within the last three months. The average 12-month AMD stock price target is $285.67, pointing to a 2.7% upside from the current price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Futures Rise on Hopes of U.S.-Iran Deal
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Friday morning as President Donald Trump said that the war in Iran "should be ending pretty soon." These remarks followed his announcement of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.39%, 0.40%, and 0.56%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on April 17.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

Oil prices fell on news of a potential resolution to the conflict in the Middle East. At the time of writing, the international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 3.47% to $95.96 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) slipped 4.12% to $90.79 per barrel.

In Thursday's regular trading session, major stock indices closed higher, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both setting fresh records. The Nasdaq Composite, S&P 500, and Dow rose 0.36%, 0.26%, and 0.24%, respectively.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) fell more than 10% in Friday's pre-market trading even after the company reported better-than-expected Q1 results. Investors were disappointed by news that co-founder and chairman Reed Hastings was stepping down from the board in June and by a weak Q2 outlook.

Meanwhile, Alcoa (AA) stock was down 2% after the aluminum producer missed earnings expectations.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Netflix (NFLX) Story Is 'Lacking Excitement,' Analyst Says as Stock Plunges 10% after Q1
data.description:
Netflix's (NFLX) shares tumbled by over 10% in Friday's pre-market session as a few analysts set Hold ratings on the streaming giant despite its first-quarter earnings beat. One of them is Pivotal Research's Jeffrey Wlodarczak, who noted that the Netflix story is "lacking excitement relative to a rich valuation."

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

During the three-month period that ended on March 31, Netflix hit a revenue of $12.3 billion, up 16% from a year ago and above the Wall Street estimate of $12.18 billion. The entertainment company also boosted its earnings per share by 86.4% from the prior-year quarter to $1.23, significantly topping analysts' estimate of $0.76.

Why Pivotal Research Is Staying Put on Netflix Stock

However, Wlodarczak, who is a four-star analyst that ranks in about the top 25% of Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks, decided to remain on the sidelines. The analyst raised his NFLX price target from $95 to $96, but this implies about 11% downside risk in the months ahead.

The Pivotal Research analyst pointed to Netflix's "weak" outlook for the ongoing second quarter. Netflix has said it expects its revenue growth to slow to 13% by the time the quarter ends on June 30.

The streaming heavyweight also projected a full-year fiscal 2026 revenue growth of between 12% and 14%, or $50.7 billion to $51.7 billion. At the midpoint of $51.2 billion, this is slightly below the Wall Street consensus of $51.38 billion.

Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan Say to Buy the Dip

However, analysts from Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan believe the post-earnings sell-off is worth buying. Morgan Stanley analyst Sean Diffley set a $115 price target that implies about 7% upside, noting that he finds Netflix's valuation as compelling "for a compounder with pricing power."

Similarly, JPMorgan's Doug Anmuth maintained his Overweight (Buy) rating on Netflix, noting that the company "continues to execute well, with considerable growth headroom." Although the analyst trimmed his price target from $120 to $118, the move still implies about 9% growth potential.

Is Netflix Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Netflix's shares remain a Strong Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down into 30 Buys and nine Holds issued over the past three months.

However, the average NFLX price target of $116.35 only implies about 8% upside.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Rival Charges into $100 Million Funding as European AI Chip Market Surges
data.description:
Euclyd, a European artificial intelligence (AI) chip startup, is raising new funding to build its technology, aiming to rival the Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) built by Nvidia (NVDA). According to Euclyd founder Bernardo Kastrup, the company is expecting to raise at least $118 million to support its growth as the AI chip market surges in Europe. 

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

Euclyd is Building Alternatives to Nvidia AI Chips

Euclyd, founded in 2024 and backed by the former CEO of ASML Holdings (ASML) Peter Wennink, is developing AI chip technology to challenge traditional GPU-based architectures. The company claims its system could deliver up to 100 times higher power efficiency for AI inference compared to Nvidia's latest chips. However, its technology is yet to be fully adopted by the AI chip industry. 

Unlike traditional GPUs that move data through memory, Euclyd's system claims to process data in multiple locations at once. This will reportedly help reduce energy use, cost, and the size of AI data centers. 

Notably, other newer companies in the sector, such as Optalysys, Fractile, and Aragon, are raising funds. Meanwhile, EU-based firms like Alexera AI and Olix have already secured investments this year.

Funding Gap and Market Challenges Remain in Europe

European AI chip startups are facing several funding and market challenges despite growing global interest in artificial intelligence hardware. This situation is largely due to Europe having a less developed semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem than in regions like the U.S. and parts of Asia, where advanced chip production is more established. 

In terms of funding, European private companies in the AI chip sector have raised about $800 million so far in 2026, significantly lower than the estimated $4.7 billion raised by their U.S. counterparts. For instance, the U.S.-based company Cerebras Systems has raised up to $1 billion in funding, highlighting a substantial gap in available venture capital between the two markets. 

Additionally, the market is highly influenced by geopolitical factors. For example, the U.S. has tightened export controls on advanced AI chips and related technologies. As a result, this has encouraged European companies to develop their domestic technologies and reduce their reliance on foreign suppliers. 

Is Nvidia a Good Stock Buy Right Now?

Wall Street analysts tracked by TipRanks rate Nvidia (NVDA) a "Strong Buy." The stock is currently trading around $198, with a price target of about $273, implying a roughly 37% upside. For more information on this stock's performance, ratings, and price targets, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Netflix Stock Crashes Post Q1 Earnings: Why Needham Says It's a 'Buy on Weakness'
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares took a hit on Friday, falling nearly 10% following its first-quarter earnings report. While the market reacted negatively to the company's outlook and a leadership change, top Needham analyst Laura Martin believes that the recent drop could offer an attractive entry point for long-term investors. Martin reiterated a Buy rating with a $120 price target, indicating over 11% upside from current levels.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

New Features to Drive NFLX's Growth

According to Martin, Netflix is evolving beyond streaming into a broader digital platform focused on user engagement. She pointed to several new products that she believes will keep users around for longer:

  • Vertical Video & Podcasts: New mobile-friendly features are designed to keep users engaged on their phones, similar to how social media apps work.
  • Kids' Games: The expansion into gaming is seen as a way to lower subscriber "churn" (the rate at which people cancel subscriptions) and give the company more power to raise prices in the future.

AI and Ads Provide an Edge

Martin also pointed to Netflix's technology advantage over traditional media peers.

The company is already using generative AI to improve content recommendations and personalize the viewing experience for its more than 325 million users.

In addition, Netflix is pushing deeper into programmatic advertising, allowing it to better monetize user engagement. As Martin noted, higher user time spent typically leads to stronger revenue growth.

Netflix Stock Dip: Is It a Buying Opportunity? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 30 Buys and nine Holds assigned in the past three months. Further, the average NFLX price target of $115.61 per share implies 7.26% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: OpenAI Debuts GPT-Rosalind, Its First AI Model Built Exclusively for Scientists
data.description:
OpenAI has launched GPT-Rosalind, a new artificial intelligence (AI) model built to support scientific and lab research. This marks the company's first AI system tailored exclusively for life sciences research, with a focus on biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

OpenAI Says GPT-Rosalind Will Speed Early Research

OpenAI described GPT-Rosalind as a life sciences model, designed to provide stronger foundational reasoning across fields such as chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. The firm noted that drug development in the U.S. takes about 10 to 15 years, with research slowed by complex workflows involving large volumes of data, the literature, and experiments.

As a result, OpenAI believes its AI model could help researchers speed up early-stage discovery by helping them accurately put evidence together, generate hypotheses, plan experiments, and support other multi-step research tasks. The private company named GPT-Rosalind after British chemist Rosalind Franklin, whose work helped reveal the structure of DNA and shaped modern molecular biology.

GPT-Rosalind Launches in ChatGPT and Codex

OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind launch comes as demand for AI tools that speed up drug discovery has increased among pharmaceutical companies, academic institutions, and biotech firms. The company said the model is now available as a research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and its API for selected customers under its trusted access system. 

In addition, the firm is rolling out a free Life Sciences research plugin for Codex that links scientists to more than 50 tools and data sources. Meanwhile, OpenAI is also working with partners including Amgen (AMGN), Moderna (MRNA), and Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to deploy GPT-Rosalind across research and development workflows.

What is the Best AI Stock to Invest in Right Now?

According to TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center, Nvidia (NVDA), Micron (MU), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) rank as the best-performing AI stocks right now. Based on Wall Street analysts' ratings over the past three months, all carry a "Strong Buy" consensus with notable upsides.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Bitcoin Bears Trapped in '2023-Style' Squeeze as Peace Talks Ignite $125,000 Price Target
data.description:
The digital asset market is on the verge of a violent upward shift as a rare combination of short-selling panic and geopolitical breakthroughs hits the tape. On Friday, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is holding firm near $74,700 after a week of gains, but the real story is hidden in the derivatives market.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

With short sellers piled into positions at levels not seen since the 2023 market bottom, analysts warn that a "forced unwind" is about to detonate a rally toward $125,000.

Trump's Peace Offensive Triggers Global Equity Records

The traditional financial world is currently pricing in a new era of stability as the White House pushes for a permanent end to Middle East hostilities.

Global stock markets are currently celebrating as President Donald Trump signals that a final agreement with Iran is "looking very good." Following the announcement of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, the S&P 500 and the MSCI All Country World Index both surged to fresh all-time highs. While the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, the war premium"on oil (CM:CL) has collapsed, with Brent crude sliding to $98.20.

This massive risk-on shift in traditional markets is providing the perfect backdrop for a crypto breakout, as institutional capital rotates out of safe havens and back into high-growth digital assets.

Short Sellers Face 'Brutal' Liquidation at $76,000 Resistance

While the spot price appears to be pausing, a massive battle is currently raging between bulls and over-leveraged bears at a key psychological level.

Bitcoin perpetual funding rates have plunged into deeply negative territory, reaching extremes last observed during the post-FTX capitulation. When funding is this negative, short sellers are literally paying long traders to keep their positions open, a sign of heavy, one-sided betting against the price.

ZeroStack CEO Daniel Reis-Faria explained that this setup creates a "powder keg" for the market. He projects that if the price breaks the current $76,000 sell wall, where $450 million in orders are waiting, the resulting short squeeze could catapult Bitcoin to $125,000 within the next two months.

Underwater Holders Create a Credibility Problem for Bitcoin's Rally

Despite the explosive potential of a short squeeze, some analysts warn that the path to six figures won't be a straight line.

Current on-chain data reveals a credibility problem for the current move. The "True Market Mean," a metric that tracks the average cost basis of active investors, suggests that the typical holder is still "underwater" on their position. This has created one of the largest gaps this year between current price action and investor sentiment.

If a short squeeze does trigger a spike to $100,000 or beyond, these underwater holders might choose to sell their bags to break even, potentially capping the rally. The ultimate direction of the market now rests on whether the U.S.-Iran peace deal is finalized before next week's deadline.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin's price is sitting at $75,508.90.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: VOO vs. VOOG vs. VOOV: Which Vanguard S&P 500 ETF Has the Most Upside in 2026?
data.description:
